purpose: Collection of four AI-powered abstract strategy board games with configurable AI engines, real-time telemetry, and responsive UIs running entirely client-side in the browser

actors.name: Human Player
actors.description: End user who plays the games via browser interface

actors.name: AI Opponent
actors.description: Configurable AI engine running in Web Workers with alpha-beta search and advanced optimizations

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Uses DevTools panels for AI tuning, telemetry analysis, and debugging

external_systems.name: Browser localStorage
external_systems.description: Persists game sessions, AI presets, UI preferences, and configuration

external_systems.name: Static Hosting (Vercel/Netlify)
external_systems.description: Serves built static assets (HTML, JS, CSS)
external_systems.note: SUPUESTO: No hay evidencia de despliegue actual en el código

containers.name: Pylos Game
containers.description: Stacking pyramid game with opening book, bitboard optimization, and phase-tapering evaluation

containers.name: Quoridor Game
containers.technology: React 19, TypeScript 5.8, Vite 7, Redux Toolkit 2.9, Tailwind CSS 4.1
containers.description: Maze-building race game with path-finding evaluation and wall pressure heuristics

containers.name: Soluna Game
containers.description: Token-merging strategy game with worker pool parallelization and adaptive time control

containers.name: Squadro Game
containers.technology: React 19, TypeScript 5.8, Vite 7, Redux Toolkit 2.9, Tailwind CSS 4.1
containers.description: Racing game with root-parallel search, DFPN endgame solver, and 12-signal evaluation function

game_engine.responsibility: Game rules, move validation, win detection, state representation

ai_engine.responsibility: Alpha-beta search, transposition table, Zobrist hashing, move ordering, evaluation, Web Worker execution

state_layer.responsibility: Global state management, action dispatching, selectors

ui_components.responsibility: Visual rendering, user interaction, responsive layouts

devtools.responsibility: AI telemetry, engine configuration, simulation runner, debugging

hooks.responsibility: AI controller, game history, localStorage persistence

main_flows.name: AI Move Calculation
main_flows.description: Critical flow: human makes move → state updates → AI triggered → Web Worker search → result returned → UI updates
steps: User clicks board to make move
State layer validates and applies move
Search request sent to Web Worker via postMessage
Worker executes iterative deepening alpha-beta search
Progress events streamed back to UI (nodes, depth, score)
Best move returned with PV, metrics, and evaluation
UI animates piece movement and updates panels

main_flows.name: AI vs AI Simulation
main_flows.description: Developer runs automated games for AI tuning and comparison
steps: Developer configures AI presets for both players
Each turn triggers AI search with configured parameters
Metrics collected: nodes, time, depth, evaluation
Results exported to CSV or visualized in charts

deployment_nodes.name: Web Browser
deployment_nodes.description: All games run entirely client-side with no backend dependencies

deployment_nodes.name: Static File Server
deployment_nodes.description: Serves Vite-built static assets (HTML, JS, CSS, assets)

deployment_nodes.name: Web Workers
deployment_nodes.description: AI computation runs off main thread to maintain UI responsiveness

assumptions: No backend server exists; all logic is client-side
Deployment target is static hosting (Vercel/Netlify) based on README roadmap
peg-solitario-game directory is empty and excluded from documentation
Python modules (MCTS, BGA scraper) are separate research tools, not part of frontend architecture
uncertainties: Exact deployment configuration not found in codebase
IndexedDB usage mentioned in utils but extent unclear
